conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex - he's 18 years old and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

This could include things like:
- A-levels or similar qualifications
- Scottish Highers
- NVQs up to level 3
- Home education (if it started before he turned 16)
- Certain training courses

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in approved education. He's studying for his A-levels at the local sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to ask:

**When did Alex turn 16, and is he still under 20 years old?**

(I can see he's 18, so under 20, but I need to confirm when he turned 16 to understand if he meets the age requirements for Child Benefit while in education.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's 18 now, so he's definitely under 20. He turned 16 two years ago when he was finishing his GCSEs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I need to clarify one more important detail about Alex's education:

**Is Alex enrolled in full-time education (more than 12 hours a week of supervised study), and has his course already started?**

Also, **does his course end before his 20th birthday, or on the week of his 20th birthday?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's in full-time education - it's definitely more than 12 hours a week. He's already started the course, he's been doing his A-levels for a while now. And yes, the course will finish before he turns 20 - A-levels are typically a two-year course, so he'll complete them well before his 20th birthday.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask about your relationship with Alex and your living situation:

**Do you live with Alex, and are you responsible for him?** (This means you're responsible for his care and upbringing, or you contribute to the costs of supporting him at a rate of at least the Child Benefit amount.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me and I'm responsible for him. I provide for all his needs and he lives with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you currently living in the UK?** And if so, do you have the right to claim benefits in the UK (for example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status or another immigration status that allows you to claim benefits)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester. And yes, I'm a UK citizen, so I have the right to claim benefits here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, the other parent or another person he might live with part of the time?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me - he lives with me full-time and no one else is getting any Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 18 years old (under 20)
✓ He's in full-time approved education (A-levels)
✓ His course will finish before he turns 20
✓ You're responsible for him and he lives with you
✓ You're a UK citizen living in the UK
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the requirements for Child Benefit. Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 (until they turn 20) if they stay in approved education or training.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. Child Benefit payments will continue until Alex finishes his A-levels or turns 20, whichever comes first.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) He is under 20 years old (currently 18); 2) He is in approved full-time education (A-levels at sixth form college with more than 12 hours per week of supervised study); 3) His course has already started and will end before his 20th birthday; 4) The claimant is responsible for Alex and he lives with them full-time; 5) The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK (Manchester) with the right to claim benefits; 6) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to gov.uk guidance, Child Benefit can continue until a young person turns 20 if they stay in approved education or training, which Alex is doing.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their son Alex. Alex is 18 years old and enrolled in full-time approved education (A-levels), which will complete before he turns 20. The claimant is responsible for Alex, lives with him in the UK, is a UK citizen with the right to claim benefits, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.
